data.title: Geely’s electric vehicle US delisting is shrewd
data.description:
The Chinese group is offering to take $6 bln Zeekr private less than a year after the unit’s New York IPO. Geely is reorganising its sprawling auto businesses to find synergies. But the price of its buyout is opportunistic and leverages US-China financial decoupling fears.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow @KatrinaHamlin on X

CONTEXT NEWS

Geely Auto on May 7 offered to take private its New York-listed electric vehicle unit Zeekr Intelligent Technology, valuing the company at $6.5 billion.

The Chinese parent offered to pay $25.66 per Zeekr American Depositary Receipt, a premium of about 14% to Zeekr’s closing price on May 6. Zeekr ADS holders can also opt to receive 12.3 newly issued Geely shares.

Geely owns 65.7% of Zeekr. After the offer was announced, Zeekr shares closed the day's trade up nearly 12% at $25.19.

data.title: NTT Data Shares Indicated Sharply Higher After Parent Considers Taking Full Control
data.description:
By Kosaku Narioka

NTT Data Group shares were indicated sharply higher after Nippon Telegraph & Telephone said it is considering acquiring shares in the information-technology services subsidiary it doesn't already own.

No shares changed hands due to overwhelming buy orders for NTT Data in Tokyo on Thursday at the limit high of 3,492 yen, a 17% jump.

NTT Data's recent earnings growth has been supported by the strength of its data-center business overseas. The company estimated in February that operating profit would increase 8.5% to Y336.00 billion, equivalent to $2.34 billion, for the fiscal year ended March.

Nippon Telegraph & Telephone said Thursday that its board is set to discuss making NTT Data a wholly owned subsidiary later in the day and that it will make an announcement if and when necessary. NTT Data also said its board will discuss the matter on Thursday. The parent holds a stake of about 58% in the subsidiary. The unit has a market capitalization of about Y4.9 trillion, factoring in Thursday's potential gains.

The companies made the comments after Japanese business daily Nikkei reported that Nippon Telegraph & Telephone planned to turn NTT Data into a wholly owned subsidiary.

Write to Kosaku Narioka at kosaku.narioka@wsj.com

data.title: BGSF Inc reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• BGSF Inc reported a quarterly adjusted loss of 7 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, identical to the same quarter last year. The mean expectation of two analysts for the quarter was for a loss of 11 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from -18 cents to -4 cents per share.

  • Revenue fell 8% to $63.23 million from a year ago; analysts expected $65.25 million.

  • BGSF Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of 7 cents​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of $722 thousand.

  • BGSF Inc shares had fallen by 8.7% this quarter and lost 35.9% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 46.7% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the employment services peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for BGSF Inc is $10.50

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data May 8 at 03:07 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: New Zealand Total Lending Increases to NZ$12.5 Billion in March
data.description:
New Zealand's total lending increased to NZ$12.5 billion in March from NZ$10.2 billion in February, according to data from the Reserve Bank of New Zealand released on Thursday.

New lending fully secured by residential mortgage jumped to NZ$8.09 billion in the month from with NZ$5.83 in the previous month.

Personal consumer new lending rose to NZ$246 million from NZ$224 million. New lending for business slid to NZ$2.91 billion from NZ$3 billion.

Lending for agriculture purposes soared to NZ$827 million from NZ$512 million. Other new lending fell to NZ$431 million from NZ$637 million.

data.title: UAE-based Aramex Posts Lower Q1 Profit
data.description:
Aramex (DFM:ARMX) said Wednesday its attributable and gross profit in the first quarter dipped year over year.

Profit attributable to equity holders of the parent for the three months ended March 31 was 17.1 million Emirati dirhams, compared with 46.6 million dirhams earlier. EPS moved to 0.012 dirham from 0.032 dirham.

The Dubai-listed transportation services company's gross profit was 364.7 million dirhams, compared with 395.4 million dirhams a year ago.

data.title: Indonesia's FX reserves fall to $152.5 bln at end-April
data.description: Indonesia's foreign exchange reserves fell by about $4.6 billion in April to $152.5 billion, enough to cover 6.4 months of imports and above international standards, the central bank said on Thursday.

data.title: Fortinet Inc reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Fortinet Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 58 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 43 cents. The mean expectation of thirty six analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 53 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 52 cents to 60 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 13.8% to $1.54 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $1.54 billion.

  • Fortinet Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was 56 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $433.4 million.

  • Fortinet Inc shares had risen by 11.3% this quarter and gained 13.4% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 0.6% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no negative revisions of earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 16 "strong buy" or "buy," 26 "hold" and 3 "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the software peer group is also "hold"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Fortinet Inc is $110.12

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data May 8 at 03:08 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Nexus Terminates Dungeon Stalkers Publishing Contract with Hive I.M
data.description:
Nexus terminated the publishing contract of the video game Dungeon Stalkers with Hive I.M upon the latter's request, the South Korean game developer said in a Thursday regulatory filing with Korea Exchange.

The contract was valued at 3 billion won.

The company did not disclose a reason for the termination of the contract.

data.title: Japan futures ease on scepticism over US-China trade talks
data.description:
• Japanese rubber futures eased on Thursday, retreating from a three-week high reached in the previous session, as investors remained sceptical that the upcoming U.S.-China trade talks would yield a breakthrough.

  • The Osaka Exchange (OSE) rubber contract for October delivery , was down 0.7 yen, or 0.23%, at 298.3 yen ($2.1) per kg as of 0246 GMT.

  • The rubber contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) for September delivery fell 225 yuan, or 1.51%, to 14,645 yuan ($2,024) per metric ton.

  • Traders were focussed on the scheduled U.S.-China trade talks in Switzerland this weekend, after months of escalating tensions that pushed tariffs between the world's two largest economies well beyond 100%. U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent will meet with China's top economic official.

  • U.S. President Donald Trump on Wednesday suggested China initiated the trade talks, adding he was not willing to cut U.S. tariffs on Chinese goods to get Beijing to negotiations. Bessent said the upcoming talks are a start, not "advanced" discussions.

  • On Wednesday, the U.S. Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged, acknowledging that risks of higher inflation and unemployment had risen, further clouding the U.S. economic outlook amid the effects of Trump's tariffs.

  • Robust demand for hybrids is expected to underpin steady profits at Toyota Motor when the world's top automaker reports annual earnings on Thursday, although investors will be on high alert for any sign of a looming impact from U.S. tariffs.

  • The yen traded at 143.51 against the U.S. dollar, compared with around 143.03 yen in late Wednesday trading in Asia.

  • Japan's Nikkei was up 0.3% on Thursday.

  • The front-month rubber contract on Singapore Exchange's SICOM platform for June delivery last traded at 171.5 U.S. cents per kg, down 0.8%.

($1 = 143.5900 yen)

($1 = 7.2342 Chinese yuan renminbi)

data.title: Lendlease Global Commercial REIT's Occupancy Remains Stable in Fiscal Q3
data.description:
Lendlease Global Commercial REIT's committed portfolio occupancy remained stable at 92.1% as at March 31, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

The REIT's tenant retention rate stood at 87.9% while rental reversion came in at 10.4% in fiscal Q3.

During the period, the REIT was also awarded a tender to redevelop a car park at Grange Road, Singapore.

The REIT also issued SG$120 million perpetual bonds at 4.75% per year to refinance SG$200 million 5.25% perpetual bonds due in April 2025.

data.title: Indonesia Forex Reserves Fall to 5-Month Low
data.description:
Indonesia’s foreign exchange reserves fell to USD 152.5 billion in April 2025, down from USD 157.1 billion in March — the highest level on record.

This marks the lowest level since last November, primarily due to the government’s external debt payments and the central bank’s ongoing efforts to stabilize the rupiah amid persistent global financial market uncertainty.

The latest figures are sufficient to cover 6.4 months of imports or 6.2 months of imports and government foreign debt payments, remaining well above the international adequacy standard of around three months of imports.

The central bank reaffirmed that foreign exchange reserves are expected to remain adequate in the coming months, supported by a positive export outlook, a surplus in the capital and financial account, and attractive investment returns.

data.title: Moody's Revises Outlook on Volvo Cars to Negative Amid Challenging Market Environment
data.description:
Moody's on Wednesday downgraded its outlook on Volvo Car (VOLCAR-B.ST), d/b/a Volvo Cars, to negative from stable, citing concerns for the company's operations amid challenging market conditions.

The rating agency said it may decide to lower the Swedish carmaker's affirmed Ba1 corporate family rating to Ba2 in the absence of operational recovery in the coming months.

data.title: Vital Healthcare Property Trust's AFFO Falls in Nine Months Ended March
data.description:
Vital Healthcare Property Trust reported Thursday that its adjusted funds from operations (AFFO) per security in the nine months to March 31 fell to NZ$7.78, compared with the prior year's NZ$8.24.

Net property income for the quarter ended March 31 was NZ$37 million, compared with NZ$36 million a year earlier.

The board also declared a quarterly distribution of NZ$0.024375 per share, unchanged from NZ$0.024375 in the previously reported period, payable June 20 to shareholders on record as of June 5.

The company also reaffirmed its fiscal year distribution guidance of NZ$0.0975 per unit.

data.title: Budweiser APAC's Q1 Profit Slides; EPS, Revenue Miss Estimates
data.description:
Budweiser Brewing APAC's attributable profit fell to $234 million in the first quarter from $287 million in the year-ago period, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

Basic earnings per share at the brewery decreased to $0.0178 from $0.0226 in the year-ago period. Visible Alpha analysts estimated EPS at $0.02.

Revenue declined 7.5% to $1.46 billion from $1.64 billion in the prior-year period. Analysts at Visible Alpha forecast revenue at $1.52 billion.

Shares slid 1% during morning trading on Thursday.

data.title: A Saudi Arabian producer offers Polyvinyl chloride (PVC) in Mozambique
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in East Africa informed that a producer from Saudi Arabia is heard to have offered its PVC resin Suspension (67S) grade at the USD 900/mt levels.This offer is for May 2025 shipment on CFR Beira port basis.

data.title: Recent offers for Polypropylene (PP Impact Copolymer) in Mozambique
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in East Africa reported that a Saudi Arabian producer is heard to have announced its offer for PP Impact Copolymer (412MK49/312MK10/312MK40/48MK40/513MN40) grades at the USD 1120-1130/mt levels.This offer is for May 2025 shipment on CFR Beira port basis.

data.title: Market Chatter: Tesla's China-Made EV Sales Drop 6% Amid Fierce Competition, Reputational Damage
data.description:
Tesla's China-made vehicle sales continued to fall for the seventh straight month, with April sales slumping 6% year on year, amid fierce competition from Chinese rivals and reputational damage in Europe, Reuters reported Wednesday.

Data from the China Passenger Car Association showed that April deliveries of Model 3 and Model Y vehicles dropped 26% month on month to 58,459 units, according to the report.

Tesla's European sales also plunged in April as Chief executive Elon Musk's far-right political endorsements alienated buyers, boosting Chinese automakers' market share. Protests against Musk in the US have further impacted Tesla's sales, Reuters wrote.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Australian Dollar Rebounds on Global Trade Optimism
data.description:
The Australian dollar climbed above $0.645 on Thursday, recovering roughly half of the previous session’s losses as optimism surrounding global trade developments lifted sentiment.

Support for the Aussie came after President Donald Trump posted on Truth Social that he would hold a press conference Thursday to announce a deal with a “big” country, reportedly the United Kingdom.

Additionally, top U.S. officials are scheduled to meet their Chinese counterparts in Switzerland this weekend in a fresh bid to ease trade tensions.

Still, Trump maintained that he would not reduce tariffs on China as a condition to begin formal negotiations.

Elsewhere, the Federal Reserve kept rates unchanged and signaled a cautious stance, highlighting uncertainty linked to tariffs.

On the domestic front, markets are increasingly pricing in a 25 basis point rate cut by the Reserve Bank of Australia at its May meeting, as signs of a slowing economy and growing external headwinds weigh on the outlook.
